summary.what: Phase 0 pending-detection diagnostic: surface side-branch latest-anchor without algorithm change
summary.why: Laura's friction on v0.22.0 read as 'pending scrambling' but root cause was the linear-anchor mental model breaking against a side-branch merge; the docSet algorithm was correct. Reviewer caught my original algorithm-change plan as a regression for solo-dev-feature-branch workflows, so this ships pure diagnostic instead — surface the topology, point at existing escape hatches, soak the data before deciding if Phase 1's algorithm change is even needed
summary.how: IsOnFirstParentLine helper does bounded first-parent walk; LatestAnchorOffFirstParent disambiguates from stale-anchor; pending and doctor wire diagnostics into JSON + human + check surfaces; new integration test codifies the regression contract for the Laura pathology; current code already passes it, which is the empirical finding driving the phased approach

notes: Independent reviewer (separate context) caught the major regression in my original plan — first-parent-aware latest entry would have lost the anchor entirely for the common 'entry on feature branch then merge' workflow that TestBranchMerge_EntryOnBranch_NoneOnMain already exercises. Their critique was specific: 'Phase 1 is wrong; drop it' with the exact counterexample. Rewrote the plan: Phase 0 diagnostic only, Phase 1 reconsidered as anchor-set termination (not anchor selection). The Phase 0 contract test passing on current code is the empirical signal that Phase 1 may not be needed at all — the friction was opacity, not incorrectness. Also surfaced and repaired two pre-existing integration test rot issues (commitEntry helper predating v0.17 auto-commit; SameDayEntries predating v0.18 filename-safe encoding) — separate commit for those.
